steps.title: Apply the small-forest rule
steps.content: The passage says that when the model is applied to small forests, its predictions for small-mammal populations are too high. Conecuh National Forest is a small forest, so that rule applies.

steps.title: Reject mismatched predictions
steps.content: Choice A concerns large mammals. Choice B introduces factors other than shorter winters. Choice D introduces predator-prey relationships, which the passage does not mention. The expected error is too-high predictions for small mammals. The answer is C.
